perturbation.original_text: 1.10. The term "Products" shall mean any in-person remote (e.g., livestream of a live event, recording of a live event, and on-demand) product or service offerings in real estate investing strategies and techniques, asset protection, and entrepreneurship in any form of communication or media to one or more recipients, including, but not limited to, workshops, seminars, webinars, coaching, and mentorships, as may be offered by LEA in the conduct of the Business and for which a fee is charged by LEA.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.10. The term "Products" shall mean offerings related to real estate investing strategies and techniques.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specificity of 'in-person or remote' and the examples like 'workshops, seminars, webinars, coaching, and mentorships,' the definition of 'Products' becomes vague. This omission creates an in-text contradiction with Section 7.1, which refers to specific types of Products like 'mobile apps,' 'podcasts,' and 'coaching programs,' implying a more detailed and comprehensive definition than is now present. This uncertainty in what constitutes a 'Product' impacts royalty calculations and the scope of the license granted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 Co-Developed Products. T&B and LEA shall meet and confer no less than quarterly to identify new Tarek's Real Estate 101 Product development, marketing and fulfillment initiatives, including, by way of example only, (i) mobile apps that provide investor resources and property evaluations, (ii) podcasts with T&B Personality that provide content to keep up to date with investing techniques and motivation, and (iii) tailored coaching programs and subscription services. The parties acknowledge that the development and fulfillment of such new Products may require substantial time and effort by the T&B Personality to fulfill such new Products such that the Marketing Royalty payable pursuant to V. B., above, is inadequate to compensate T&B Personality; therefore, in lieu of any other royalty, the parties shall share Cash Sales from the sale of such new Products as follows: [●%] to LEA [●%] to T&B
perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly narrows the definition of 'Products' by removing 'in-person or remote' and specific examples like 'workshops, seminars, webinars, coaching, and mentorships.' This directly contradicts Section 7.1, which refers to 'mobile apps,' 'podcasts,' and 'coaching programs' as examples of 'Products.' The contradiction arises because the changed definition creates ambiguity about whether these specific examples still fall under the definition of 'Products,' impacting the royalty calculations and scope of the license, creating an in-text contradiction under the Omissions definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1.1 Base Royalty. In consideration of the License granted and other good and valuable consideration provided by T&B to LEA, LEA shall pay to T&B a base royalty ("Base Royalty") in the amount of [●%] of LEA's monthly Cash Sales for Cash Sales of up to [$●]. For monthly Cash Sales above [$●] and up to [$●] , the Base Royalty paid to T&B by LEA shall be [●%]of the LEA's Cash Sales. For monthly Cash Sales above [$●] and up to [$●] the Base Royalty paid to T&B by LEA shall be [●%] of the LEA's Cash Sales. For monthly Cash Sales above [$●] and up to [$●], the Base Royalty paid to T&B by LEA shall be [●%] of the LEA's Cash Sales. For monthly Cash Sales above [$●]the Base Royalty paid to T&B by LEA shall be [●%] of the LEA's Cash Sales. Payments will be made in U.S. Dollars.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1.1 Base Royalty. In consideration of the License granted and other good and valuable consideration provided by T&B to LEA, LEA shall pay to T&B a base royalty ("Base Royalty"). Payments will be made in U.S. Dollars.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the specific royalty percentages and cash sales tiers creates an in-text contradiction with the intent of Section 9 (Royalties and Reporting). The section continues to discuss 'Base Royalty' calculations and reporting obligations, implying a defined royalty structure that no longer exists due to the omission. This makes the enforcement of royalty payments uncertain, as the fundamental basis for calculation is missing. Specifically, section 9.4 reffers to Royalty Payment that is being removed, which is generating that in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.4. For each Base Royalty and Marketing Royalty payment (collectively, "Royalty Payment"), LEA shall render to T&B, a written statement, in such form as T&B may reasonably request, setting forth Cash Sales made during the period to which the Royalty Payment relates, and such other information as T&B may reasonably request to verify the Royalty Payments due hereunder. LEA shall keep such written records respecting Cash Sales as T&B may reasonably request so that Royalty Payments payable hereunder may be accurately determined and shall permit such records to be examined by T&B or its authorized representative upon reasonable prior written notice at any reasonable time during regular business hours to verify the records, reports and payments herein provided.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific royalty percentages and cash sales tiers, which directly contradicts section 9.4. Section 9.4 explicitly refers to 'Base Royalty and Marketing Royalty payment' and requires LEA to provide written statements setting forth 'Cash Sales made during the period to which the Royalty Payment relates' to verify 'Royalty Payments due hereunder'. Since the royalty calculation basis is removed in the changed text, it's impossible to accurately determine or verify royalty payments as required by section 9.4, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The modified text directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section and makes enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 11.2 The Agreement may be terminated: (i) immediately by either party in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the other party that is susceptible of cure and such breach is not cured within the 30-day period after written notice of such breach to the breaching party. (ii) by either party, immediately, if the other party becomes insolvent, makes an assignment for the benefit of its creditors, or becomes the subject of any bankruptcy or insolvency proceedings, and such proceedings are not vacated within sixty (60) days of their initiation.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.2 The Agreement may be terminated: (i) immediately by either party in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the other party that is susceptible of cure and such breach is not cured within the 30-day period after written notice of such breach to the breaching party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause (ii) regarding termination upon insolvency or bankruptcy, the contract creates an in-text contradiction. Other sections, such as 11.5, which address sections that survive termination, may indirectly rely on a comprehensive understanding of all termination scenarios. By removing one of the termination reasons, this may create ambiguity when the insolvency happens, since the other sections rely on a more comprehensive understanding of all termination events. Because there is a section that survives this termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.5 Sections 4.2; 8 (Confidentiality); 10 (Warranties and Representations); 12 (Indemnification); and 14 (Miscellaneous) hereof shall survive the expiration or early termination (for any reason) of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the termination clause related to insolvency or bankruptcy in Section 11.2 creates an in-text contradiction with Section 11.5. Section 11.5 specifies that certain sections survive 'early termination (for any reason)'. By omitting insolvency as a termination reason, the contract becomes ambiguous about whether those sections survive an insolvency event. The original contract comprehensively covered termination events, and the contradicted text depends on that comprehensive understanding. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.
